# Development Log - 2026-06-14

## Changes

- **[recurrence handler — delete "this"]**: Scope-"this" delete now emits exactly one persistence callback. When a stored override is dropped, only `deleted` is reported; otherwise only `updated` (new EXDATE on the base). In-memory state still always applies the EXDATE and removes the override row so the occurrence stays excluded even when the callback is delete-only.

## Files Modified

- `packages/plugins/recurrence/src/utils/delete-recurring-event.ts` - Mutually exclusive `updated` / `deleted` reporting keyed on `droppedOverride`
- `packages/plugins/recurrence/src/utils/recurring-mutations.test.ts` - Override delete expects delete-only callback
- `packages/plugins/recurrence/src/utils/crud-operations.test.ts` - Added override vs generated-instance callback test
- `packages/calendar/src/features/calendar/contexts/calendar-context/provider.test.tsx` - Updated recurring delete callback expectations
- `packages/calendar/src/features/calendar/hooks/use-calendar-engine.test.ts` - Added override delete integration test

## Notes

Previous attempt skipped EXDATE mutation when the target was an override, which could leave the base without an exclusion and make the occurrence reappear. The correct fix is to keep mutating the store but gate the callbacks: override present → `onEventDelete` only; no override → `onEventUpdate` only.

## Changes (fix #197 — recurring override lost on re-render)

- **[calendar — IlamyCalendar]**: Memoized `normalizeEvents(events)` with `useMemo([events])`. It was called inline in render, so every `IlamyCalendar` re-render produced a new array reference; `use-calendar-data`'s events-prop sync (`events !== lastEventsProp.current`) then reset internal `currentEvents`, discarding in-memory edits (scope-"this" overrides/EXDATEs, drags). In the demo, `initialDate` is controlled (updates on navigation), so navigating re-rendered `IlamyCalendar` and reverted the edited occurrence. Memoizing keeps the array stable while the consumer's `events` prop is unchanged.

## Files Modified (fix #197)

- `packages/calendar/src/features/calendar/components/ilamy-calendar.tsx` - `useMemo` the normalized events.
- `packages/calendar/src/features/calendar/components/ilamy-calendar.test.tsx` - Regression test: a scope-"this" edit survives an `IlamyCalendar` re-render (uses `headerComponent` to drive the edit from context, then `rerender`).
- `docs/logs/2026-05-30.md` - Deleted (oldest, keeping the log dir at the 10-file cap).

## Notes (fix #197)

- Root cause was a referential-stability bug, not the recurrence handler. Verified the recurrence/transform + engine layers persist overrides across navigation when the events prop is stable; the new array each render was the trigger.
- Separate (not fixed here): the `use-calendar-data` sync still discards internal edits whenever a consumer legitimately changes the `events` prop. Defensible for controlled events, but worth revisiting.

## Changes (drop inline sourcemaps from private packages)

- **[build]**: Removed `sourcemap: true` from the bunup configs of the private, bundled packages (`@ilamy/ui`, `@ilamy/utils`, `@ilamy/calendar-recurrence`, `@ilamy/calendar-agenda`). In bunup `true` means *inline* base64 maps embedded in every `.js` (verified in bunup 0.16.32: `sourcemap === true` → `"inline"`). These packages are private and bundled into `@ilamy/calendar` from source, so their dist (and its maps) is never shipped or used at runtime; the inline maps were pure bloat. Omitting the option resolves to no sourcemap. The published `@ilamy/calendar` already used `sourcemap: 'none'`.

## Files Modified (sourcemaps)

- `packages/ui/bunup.config.ts`, `packages/utils/bunup.config.ts`, `packages/plugins/recurrence/bunup.config.ts`, `packages/plugins/agenda/bunup.config.ts` - Dropped `sourcemap: true`.

## Notes (sourcemaps)

- Verified: full build succeeds and no `sourceMappingURL=data:...base64` remains in any private dist.
- Documented in `AGENTS.md`: run Fallow locally with `--no-cache`. Without it, `--changed-since` registers a per-base-sha worktree under `$TMPDIR` (Fallow's base-snapshot cache) that accumulates in `git worktree list`; `--no-cache` leaves none. CI (the Fallow action on an ephemeral runner) is unaffected.

## Changes (agenda window refinements — single-view "Schedule" model)

Followed #139's intent: the agenda is one chronological upcoming-events list (Google Calendar "Schedule"), not a set of day/week/month agenda views. Briefly prototyped multi-window agenda + a grouped/dropdown switcher, but it put a second Day/Week/Month next to the grid views' Day/Week/Month, which read as confusing duplication. Reverted that; kept the window as a single developer config and the genuinely-good fixes below.

- **[agenda — single window config]**: `agendaPlugin({ window })` / `createAgendaView({ window })` registers one `agenda` view (unchanged API). `window: AgendaWindow = 'day' | 'week' | 'month' | number`, default `'month'`. Named periods are the calendar period *containing* the current date (so 'month' on the 14th lists from the 1st, matching the grid view + header); a number is a rolling window of N days *from* the current date forward (e.g. `8` on the 14th lists the 14th–21st). This named-vs-numeric anchoring is intentional and documented on `AgendaWindow` + `AgendaViewOptions.window`.
- **[agenda — fdow-aware week]**: The `week` window aligns to `config.firstDayOfWeek` (`createAgendaView`'s `range` passes it through; `AgendaView` threads `firstDayOfWeek` from context into `windowRange`).
- **[agenda — dropped year window]**: Removed `'year'` from `AgendaWindow` (a year-long event list is unhelpful).
- **[agenda — empty state]**: Centered the empty-window message (`flex h-full items-center justify-center`) so a short window (e.g. a day) doesn't pin "No upcoming events" to the corner. Sparse non-empty lists still flow from the top (reads like an inbox).

## Files Modified (agenda window refinements)

- `packages/plugins/agenda/src/utils/agenda-window.ts` - Removed `year`; documented named-vs-numeric anchoring; `windowRange` takes `firstDayOfWeek` for the week window.
- `packages/plugins/agenda/src/utils/create-agenda-view.ts` - `range` passes `config.firstDayOfWeek`; expanded the `window` doc.
- `packages/plugins/agenda/src/components/agenda-view.tsx` - `firstDayOfWeek` from context into `windowRange`; centered empty state.
- `packages/plugins/agenda/src/utils/create-agenda-view.test.ts` - Added day-window and fdow-aware week-window range tests.
- `packages/plugins/agenda/src/components/agenda-view.test.tsx` - `renderAgenda` takes a window; added a day-window event-scoping test.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-data.ts` - Replaced the module-level `demoPlugins` with `createDemoPlugins(agendaWindow)` so the window can vary at runtime.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-page.tsx` - Added `agendaWindow` state (default 'week') + a `useMemo` plugins array (stable unless the window changes); threads `plugins` to the display and `agendaWindow`/`setAgendaWindow` to settings.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-calendar-settings.tsx` - Added an "Agenda Window" Select (Day/Week/Month/Next 3 days/Next 14 days), shown for the regular calendar; `parseAgendaWindow` maps the string value back to a named period or numeric window.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-calendar-display.tsx` - Takes `plugins` as a prop (threaded to both calendar variants) instead of importing the module-level constant.

Live update works without a remount: `use-calendar-engine` derives the plugin runtime via `useMemo(() => createPluginRuntime(plugins), [plugins])`, so a new memoized plugins array re-registers the agenda view with the new window while navigation state is preserved.

## Notes (agenda window refinements)

- Reviewed FullCalendar (listDay/listWeek/listMonth as selectable views) and Full Event Calendar (a List/Calendar display-mode toggle orthogonal to day/week/month). Both keep agenda at multiple granularities, but #139 and its one real consumer asked for a single upcoming-events list with a *developer-configured* window (`createAgendaView({ days })`), not an end-user granularity switcher. So agenda stays a single view; the FEC display-mode toggle would be a switcher rebuild for a capability nobody requested (YAGNI), and it drags in a year-has-no-list-counterpart edge.
- Reverted in full: `PluginView.group`, the `windows[]` plugin option, the grouped/Popover switcher in `view-controls.tsx`, and the multi-window demo wiring. No public-API change versus main for the switcher or the plugin option.

## Changes (demo settings — react-hook-form + reusable form components)

Simplified the demo settings panel. All ~28 settings live in one react-hook-form; `FormProvider` wraps the whole `DemoPage`, and both the settings panel and the calendar display read what they need from context. No props are threaded for settings (was ~60 on the panel, ~30 on the display).

- **[demo — form components]**: New `FormSelect`, `FormInput`, `FormCheckbox` (in `apps/demo/.../form/`), each a thin `useController` + `useFormContext` wrapper around the matching `@ilamy/ui` primitive. `FormSelect` takes an `options: { value: string; label: string }[]` prop (renders the `SelectItem`s itself). `FormSelect`/`FormInput` take an optional `parse` (e.g. `Number`, `parseAgendaWindow`) to convert the control's string value to the stored form value. Option lists live as named constants in `demo-calendar-settings.tsx`.
- **[demo — single form]**: `demo-settings-form.ts` defines `DemoSettingsValues` + `defaultSettings`. `DemoCalendarSettings` takes no props; `useWatch('calendarType')` drives the resource-only sections. Business start/end became number `FormInput`s (were 24-item selects).
- **[demo — display reads context]**: `DemoCalendarDisplay` no longer receives settings as props — it pulls them via `useWatch({ control })` and derives plugins/businessHours/scrollTime/calendarKey itself. It takes only the non-form data (`customEvents`, `resourceEvents`, `activeResources`). `DemoPage` keeps just one `useWatch('calendarType')` (to toggle the resource picker) and no longer re-renders on every field change.
- **[demo — useWatch over watch]**: Used `useWatch` throughout instead of `form.watch()`. `useWatch({ control })` with no name returns a deep-partial (every field optional), unlike `form.watch()` which returns the full type; the display merges it over `defaultSettings` (`{ ...defaultSettings, ...watched }`) to recover a fully-defined object.
- **[demo — special fields]**: Initial Date and Hidden Days bind via `useController` directly (a preset Select over an ISO string, and a checkbox group over an array). `initialDate` is stored as an ISO string, not a Dayjs — react-hook-form's path-value typing resolves the Dayjs class to `never`, so the form holds the string and the display / the Initial Date control convert at the boundaries.

## Files Modified (demo react-hook-form)

- `apps/demo/package.json` - Added `react-hook-form@^7.79.0` (peer-compatible with React 19).
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/form/{form-select,form-input,form-checkbox}.tsx` - New reusable RHF-bound controls.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-settings-form.ts` - New `DemoSettingsValues` type + `defaultSettings`.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-calendar-settings.tsx` - Rewritten to consume the form via context; no props.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-calendar-display.tsx` - Reads settings via `useWatch`; derives plugins/businessHours/scrollTime/calendarKey + `onDateChange` (via `setValue`) internally; props reduced to `customEvents`/`resourceEvents`/`activeResources`.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-page.tsx` - One `useForm` wrapping the page in `FormProvider`; only `useWatch('calendarType')` for the resource-picker toggle.

## Notes (demo react-hook-form)

- The form components use `useFormContext()` (untyped `FieldValues`) + `name: string` and coerce at the boundary (`String(field.value)`, `Boolean(field.value)`), so call sites stay free of per-control generics while avoiding any explicit `any`.

## Changes (demo app — bulletproof folder structure)

The demo app had everything dumped in one `src/components/demo/` folder, including non-components. Reorganized by type at the top level (the app *is* the demo, so no `demo` feature/folder layer).

- **[demo — structure]**: `src/components/demo/*` → organized by type: components in `src/components/` (`calendar-display`, `calendar-settings`, `resource-picker`, `mode-toggle`, `form/*`), non-components out of `components/` (`src/utils/demo-data.ts`, `src/utils/custom-renderers.tsx`, `src/types/settings-form.ts`). Dropped the redundant `demo-` filename prefix and `Demo*` component-name prefix (`DemoCalendarDisplay` → `CalendarDisplay`, etc.).
- **[demo — App is the page]**: `DemoPage` absorbed into `App.tsx` (no router, one screen), so the separate page wrapper is gone. Layout markup unchanged.
- **[demo — dayjs locales]**: the ~140 `import 'dayjs/locale/*'` side-effect lines moved out of the page into `src/lib/dayjs-locales.ts`, imported once by `App.tsx`.
- **[config — biome]**: the two demo-scoped overrides keyed on `**/src/components/demo/**` (turning off `a11y/noLabelWithoutControl`, excluding the `no-inline-template-keys` plugin) now key on `apps/demo/**`. The `keys.*` factory that plugin requires is a calendar-package internal not reachable from the demo, so the whole demo app stays excluded.

## Files Modified (demo restructure)

- `apps/demo/src/components/{calendar-display,calendar-settings,resource-picker,mode-toggle}.tsx`, `apps/demo/src/components/form/*` - Moved out of `components/demo/`; components renamed to drop the `Demo` prefix.
- `apps/demo/src/utils/{demo-data.ts,custom-renderers.tsx}`, `apps/demo/src/types/settings-form.ts` - Non-components relocated by type.
- `apps/demo/src/lib/dayjs-locales.ts` - New: extracted locale side-effect imports.
- `apps/demo/src/App.tsx` - Absorbed `DemoPage`; imports the locale module.
- `apps/demo/src/components/demo/demo-page.tsx` - Deleted.
- `biome.json` - Demo overrides retargeted from `**/src/components/demo/**` to `apps/demo/**`.

## Changes (import docs website as apps/website)

Brought the standalone docs site (`../ilamy.io`, deployed at ilamy.dev) into the monorepo as `apps/website` (`@ilamy/website`, private). Astro + Starlight. Copied via `git archive HEAD | tar -x` (tracked files only — structurally cannot pull in an untracked `.env`/secret); dropped the repo's own `bun.lock`, `biome.json`, `.claude`, `.github`, `.vscode`, `copilot-instructions.md`.

- **[website — workspace link]**: Replaced the published-version / `.local-pkg` tarball dance (`use-local-calendar.sh`, `calendar:local`) with `"@ilamy/calendar": "workspace:*"`. The docs now build against the in-repo calendar, so API drift shows up in the docs build in the same PR.
- **[website — deps all latest]**: Bumped every dependency to its current latest. One forced exception: **Vite stays `^7`** because Astro 6.4.6 peer-requires `vite ^7` (no Astro-on-Vite-8 release exists); the demo keeps its own Vite 8. bun resolves Vite 7 nested for the website.
- **[website — API drift fixed]** (surfaced by building against the workspace calendar):
  - `RRule` moved to `@ilamy/calendar/plugins/recurrence` (v2 split) — updated `seed.ts`.
  - lucide-react v1 dropped brand icons → added `icons/github-icon.tsx` (local SVG) for the nav bar.
  - react-day-picker v10 renamed the `table` classNames key to `month_grid` — updated `ui/calendar.tsx`.
  - The v2 i18n dropped weekday/short-day/month translation keys and added 13 new ones. Reworked `lib/translations` to overlay locale overrides on the calendar's exported `defaultTranslations` (`{...defaultTranslations, ...overrides}`), typing each locale file `Partial<Translations>`, so future key changes never break the docs.
  - Switched 5 files from `import dayjs from "dayjs"` to `import { dayjs } from "@ilamy/calendar"` (the configured instance, per the repo's `noRestrictedImports` rule) and the `dayjs.Dayjs` namespace types to the imported `Dayjs` type.
  - Deleted dead `code-example.tsx` (imported the non-dependency `prismjs`); exported `ButtonProps` from `ui/button.tsx` (pre-existing missing export).
- **[config]**: Root `biome.json` demo overrides broadened to `apps/**` (the keys-factory plugin is library-internal). Root `build` script appends `--filter '@ilamy/website' build` after the calendar; added `dev:website`. Biome reformatted the imported site to house style (tabs/single-quote).
- **[deploy]**: `deploy` script = `astro build && wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name="$CLOUDFLARE_PAGES_PROJECT" --branch=main`. Manual Cloudflare Pages deploy; auth via `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` + `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` env vars (never committed — public repo). Documented in `apps/website/README.md`.

## Files Modified (docs import)

- `apps/website/**` - New: the imported Astro/Starlight docs site (reworked as above).
- `package.json` - `dev:website` + website in the build chain.
- `biome.json` - demo/website overrides scoped to `apps/**`.

## Notes (docs import)

- Security: copied with `git archive` (tracked only); scanned the tree for secrets/`.env` (none); `.gitignore` covers `.env*`. Cloudflare creds are env-only.
- Pre-existing warning (not new): `ical-export.mdx` uses an ```` ```icalendar ```` fence; shiki has no `icalendar` grammar so it falls back to `txt`. Cosmetic.
- Manual follow-ups for the user: set the `CLOUDFLARE_*` env vars locally; disable the Pages project's Git integration so push-to-main stops auto-deploying; archive the standalone `calendar.ilamy.dev` repo.
- Verification: full `build` chain (…calendar → demo → website), `type-check` (all workspaces, 0 errors), `test` (calendar 786 / recurrence 165 / agenda 19), `check` (biome clean), Fallow new-only over 102 files (clean).

## Changes (consolidate the demo into a shared @ilamy/playground package)

The demo UI existed twice — `apps/demo` (clean react-hook-form version) and the website's `src/components/pages/demo/*` (older, useState, feature-rich). Consolidated into one source-only package, `@ilamy/playground` (`packages/playground`, private, never built/published), consumed by both apps so it's maintained in one place.

- **[playground — package]**: New `@ilamy/playground` with `"exports": { ".": "./src/index.ts" }` (source-only). Holds the RHF framework (calendar-display, calendar-settings, FormSelect/Input/Checkbox, settings-form, demo-data, custom-renderers, seed, dayjs-locales) + the top-level `Playground` component. No bunup/build — both apps resolve it to source.
- **[playground — full website parity]**: Kept the demo app's clean RHF architecture but added the website demo's full feature set: `calendarType`/orientation/weekViewGranularity, business hours (enable + day picker + start/end), eventSpacing, hideExportButton, and the custom-renderer/behavior demos (custom calendar header, custom event form, resource renderer, current-time indicator, hour renderer, event lifecycle add/update/delete). Lifecycle callbacks mutate the Playground's event state so add/update/delete reflect live. Uses `IlamyCalendar` + `resources` (not the deprecated `IlamyResourceCalendar`).
- **[demo app]**: `App.tsx` is now a thin host — `<ModeToggle /> + <Playground />`. `vite.config` aliases `@ilamy/playground` (+ the other `@ilamy/*`) to source for Fast Refresh. Dropped `react-hook-form`/`rrule` deps (moved to the playground).
- **[website]**: `/demo` mounts `<Playground client:only="react" />`; deleted the old `pages/demo/*`. Resolves `@ilamy/playground` (+ its transitive `@ilamy/ui`) to source via tsconfig paths, while `@ilamy/calendar` stays dist (preserves packaging validation). Tailwind `@source`s the playground + ui src.
- **[playground — relative imports]**: The playground uses relative imports internally (not `@/`); a consumer's tsc applies its own `@/` mapping to imported source, so a self-alias breaks the demo's type-check. Relative paths resolve regardless of which tsconfig is active.
- **[config]**: `biome.json` extends the `apps/**` exemptions (a11y label-wrap + the keys plugin) to `packages/playground/**`. `fallow.toml` adds `packages/playground/**` to `[health]`/`[duplicates]` ignores (demo branching). The shipped library packages are still fully gated.

## Files Modified (playground consolidation)

- `packages/playground/**` - New source-only demo package (moved from `apps/demo/src`, expanded to website parity).
- `apps/demo/src/App.tsx`, `apps/demo/{package.json,vite.config.ts}` - Thin host + playground alias/dep.
- `apps/website/src/pages/demo.astro`, `apps/website/{package.json,tsconfig.json}`, `apps/website/src/styles/globals.css` - Mount the shared Playground; resolve playground + ui to source; deleted `src/components/pages/demo/*`.
- `biome.json`, `fallow.toml` - Exempt `packages/playground/**` from app-style lint/health gating.

## Notes (playground consolidation)

- Demo app keeps Fast Refresh (source alias). Website consumes the playground as source too, but `@ilamy/calendar` stays dist there, so the website still validates the published calendar package surface.
- Verification: `type-check` (all workspaces, 0 errors), `test` (calendar 786 / recurrence 165 / agenda 19), `build` (full chain + both apps), `check` (biome clean), Fallow new-only over 29 files (clean).

### Settings UI updated to match the website design

- Calendar Mode / Resource Orientation / Week View Granularity render as segmented button groups (new reusable `FormButtonGroup`, selected = `secondary`, others = `ghost`), with a helper line under granularity.
- Weekday pickers (business days + hidden days) are a two-column, Monday-first grid with short labels (Mon/Tue/…).
- Business hours: the enabled block is indented under a left border; Start/End are time selects ("9 AM (09:00)") instead of number inputs; the block precedes Hidden Days.
- Removed `form-input.tsx` (no longer used now that times are selects).
